entries.definition:
m–k–k to suck the mother’s milk dry, to chew on hard bones;
crowdedness; to press a debtor for a debt; dry measure, drinking
goblet; to chirp. Of this root, only مَكَّة makkah occurs once in the
Qur’an.

مَكَّة Makkah [proper name] Mecca (48:24) وَهُوَ الَّذِي كَفَّ أَيْدِيَهُمْ عَنْكُمْ

891
